reasoning: No norovirus or sapovirus vaccine is currently approved. The leading candidates are still deep in clinical trials. Moderna's Phase 3 trial for its norovirus vaccine was just announced (on November 6, 2025) to be extending into the 2025-2026 Northern Hemisphere winter season due to an inability to accrue enough cases during the Southern Hemisphere season. Vaxart is planning a Phase 3 trial that will begin no earlier than 2026. HilleVax experienced a Phase 2b trial failure in 2024 and was restructuring its approach in 2025. Given these timelines, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to complete Phase 3, secure regulatory approval, and be documented as "Yes" on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page by February 2026. Therefore, the probability is set near the 2% minimum, slowly increasing over the resolution dates to account for the slight chance of Wikipedia vandalism or an abrupt foreign vaccine launch.
